name: Ensure IAM Users that are inactive for 30 days or more are deactivated
body:
### Overview

Cyscale fails this control when AWS IAM users have not been used for 30 days or more. Long-inactive users are often forgotten standing access and should be disabled, removed, or converted to federated access.

The next assessment will pass after inactive IAM users are deactivated or removed from the account.
description: AWS IAM users that have been inactive for 30 days or more.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Disable or remove inactive IAM user access.

1. Review the affected IAM user and confirm whether it is still required.
2. If the user is not required, delete access keys, remove the console password, detach policies, and delete the user.
3. If the user is temporarily required, rotate credentials and document an exception with an expiry date.
4. Prefer IAM Identity Center or federated role access instead of long-lived IAM users.

CLI review commands:

```bash
aws iam get-user --user-name {{asset.name}}
aws iam list-access-keys --user-name {{asset.name}}
```

Disable console password and access keys:

```bash
aws iam delete-login-profile --user-name {{asset.name}}
aws iam update-access-key --user-name {{asset.name}} --access-key-id <access-key-id> --status Inactive
```

Cyscale will pass when the user is no longer active or no longer present in the AWS IAM inactive-user query.
